clusters.title: New IOC policy bans transgender women from Olympics
clusters.neutral_headline: IOC Bans Transgender Women From Olympics

items.title: New IOC policy bans transgender women from Olympics
items.standfirst: A new policy announced by the International Olympic Committee bans transgender women athletes from the Olympics. NBC News Dana Griffin explains the new rule.
items.content: A new policy announced by the International Olympic Committee bans transgender women athletes from the Olympics. NBC News Dana Griffin explains the new rule.

items.title: The Olympic committee bans trans athletes from women's events, raising many questions
items.standfirst: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.
items.content: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles. (Image credit: Leon Neal)

items.title: Transgender women banned from competing in Olympic games
items.standfirst: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) on Thursday banned transgender women from competing in games, and said only "biological females" will be allowed to participate in women's events. The IOC is reintroducing testing for gender to determine eligibility, which will also rule out many athletes with differences in sexual development.
items.content: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) on Thursday banned transgender women from competing in games, and said only "biological females" will be allowed to participate in women's events. The IOC is reintroducing testing for gender to determine eligibility, which will also rule out many athletes with differences in sexual development.

items.title: Olympic women's sport limited to biological females
items.standfirst: The women's category of Olympic sports will be limited to biological females from 2028, says the International Olympic Committee.
items.content: The women's category of Olympic sports will be limited to biological females from 2028, says the International Olympic Committee.

ai_summary: IOC announces new policy banning transgender women athletes
Policy requires genetic testing for women's events
Rule takes effect for 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles
Only biological females will be allowed in women's events
IOC reintroduces testing for gender to determine eligibility

clusters.title: Which U.S. troops are heading to the Middle East, and what will they be doing?

items.standfirst: President Trump extended his deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz before he orders strikes on the country's power plants. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

items.title: Trump pushes back deadline for Iran to open Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: President Trump is pushing back his ultimatum, at Iran's request, for the country to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, or face U.S. strikes on its power plants. It comes as the president said negotiations are underway, even as thousands of troops head to the Middle East. CBS News' Holly Williams and Zak Hudak report.
items.content: President Trump is pushing back his ultimatum, at Iran's request, for the country to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, or face U.S. strikes on its power plants. It comes as the president said negotiations are underway, even as thousands of troops head to the Middle East. CBS News' Holly Williams and Zak Hudak report.

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump says he is ‘pausing’ planned destruction of Iranian energy sites as he claims talks are ‘ongoing’
items.standfirst: US president says he is extending deadline to Monday 6 April as he claims Iran has let some tankers through strait of Hormuz as a ‘present’Analysis: Trump pitches peace plan but military buildups rarely veer to off-rampAn Iranian envoy has said South Korean ships can pass through the strait of Hormuz only after coordinating with Tehran, the Yonhap News Agency has reported.Such an agreement had to be reached in advance of the transit, said Saeed Khuzechi, the Iranian ambassador to South Korea, at a press conference in response to a question about guarantees for South Korean vessels to navigate the vital conduit for oil. items.title: Trump extends deadline for Iran to open strait of Hormuz by 10 days
items.standfirst: President claims talks with Tehran regime are ‘going very well’ and says he is pausing ‘Energy Plant destruction’Middle East crisis – live updatesDonald Trump has extended his deadline for Iran to open the strait of Hormuz by 10 days to 6 April after saying talks are “going very well”.The president made the statement on Thursday in a social media post, saying: “As per Iranian Government request, please let this statement serve to represent that I am pausing the period of Energy Plant destruction by 10 Days to Monday, April 6, 2026, at 8 P.M., Eastern Time,” Trump said on his Truth Social platform. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Jury Finds Meta, Google Liable for Addiction | Bloomberg Tech 3/26/2026
clusters.neutral_headline: Meta And Google Liable For Addiction

items.title: Jury Finds Meta, Google Liable for Addiction | Bloomberg Tech 3/26/2026
items.standfirst: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss the jury verdict holding Google and Meta liable for a young woman’s social media addiction. Plus, the case in California was just the start of thousands of similar cases that could lead to impacts on the businesses of social media companies. And Google researchers tout a new compression technique for LLMs and vector search engines, sending shares of memory and storage companies lower. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss the jury verdict holding Google and Meta liable for a young woman’s social media addiction. Plus, the case in California was just the start of thousands of similar cases that could lead to impacts on the businesses of social media companies. And Google researchers tout a new compression technique for LLMs and vector search engines, sending shares of memory and storage companies lower. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: How a Social Media Trial Verdict Threatens Big Tech
items.standfirst: A flood of lawsuits filed in recent years against social media giants claim that by engineering their platforms to be hard to resist, the companies are causing serious harm to young users. In the first case to go to trial, a jury on March 25 found Meta Platforms Inc. and Google negligent in the design and operation of their platforms – a landmark verdict.
items.content: A flood of lawsuits filed in recent years against social media giants claim that by engineering their platforms to be hard to resist, the companies are causing serious harm to young users. In the first case to go to trial, a jury on March 25 found Meta Platforms Inc. and Google negligent in the design and operation of their platforms – a landmark verdict.

items.standfirst: Research suggested resurgence in Christianity, especially among young people, but some respondents found to be fraudulentA YouGov survey showing a significant rise in church attendance in parts of the UK has been withdrawn after some respondents were found to be fraudulent.The poll was central to a Quiet Revival report, published by the Bible Society last year, which prompted news stories about an apparent resurgence in Christianity, particularly among young people. items.standfirst: US president says he is ‘very disappointed’ as he again lashes out at allies’ lack of involvement in Iran warMiddle East crisis – live updatesDonald Trump has dismissed British warships as “toys” in his latest jibe at Nato countries for their lack of involvement in the joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Speaking at the White House on Thursday, he claimed he had told the UK: “Don’t bother, we don’t need it.”Trump has previously alleged that he requested two aircraft carriers from the UK that Keir Starmer had initially rejected and then offered to send. items.title: UN votes to describe slave trade as ‘gravest crime against humanity’
items.standfirst: Members call for reparatory justice as landmark resolution aims for ‘political recognition at the highest level’The United Nations has voted to describe the transatlantic chattel slave trade as the “gravest crime against humanity” and called for reparations as “a concrete step towards remedying historical wrongs”.The landmark resolution passed on Wednesday was backed by the African Union (AU) and the Caribbean Community (Caricom). It had been proposed by Ghana’s president, John Dramani Mahama, who said: “Let it be recorded that when history beckoned, we did what was right for the memory of millions who suffered the indignity of slavery.” Continue reading...
items.content: Members call for reparatory justice as landmark resolution aims for ‘political recognition at the highest level’The United Nations has voted to describe the transatlantic chattel slave trade as the “gravest crime against humanity” and called for reparations as “a concrete step towards remedying historical wrongs”.The landmark resolution passed on Wednesday was backed by the African Union (AU) and the Caribbean Community (Caricom). It had been proposed by Ghana’s president, John Dramani Mahama, who said: “Let it be recorded that when history beckoned, we did what was right for the memory of millions who suffered the indignity of slavery.” Continue reading...
